Title: D0030 wrapper code implemented
Body: Added optional magic_link to ams_send and ams_recv inputSchema, reordered handler auth checks to mirror D0029's ams_join pattern, implemented SessionDO ensureWire self-rehydration with concurrent coalescing, left-state suppression, and buffer-gap truncation notification.

Title: Baseline at SHA 3066f73 confirmed post-#40/#69
Body: synthesizeFromMagicLink reused from D0029 (not duplicated). account_is_transient: true set on all synthesis paths. Two-door gate at ams_create_conversation unchanged. deriveStreamId in conversation.ts called by Conversation DO — wrapper passes same tuple through ensureWire → dialWire.

Title: deriveStreamId indirection via Conversation DO
Body: deriveStreamId lives in conversation.ts and is called by the Conversation DO during dialWire — the wrapper does not need to call it directly. The deterministic stream_id property is preserved by passing the same (account_id, stream_name, conversation_id) tuple through ensureWire → dialWire → Conversation DO.

Title: No ams_leave tool exists
Body: hasLeft field is wired but currently always false. Left-state suppression is structurally correct but not exercisable until ams_leave ships. Not in scope for D0030.

Title: readyState check conservative interpretation
Body: D0030 contract says "wireWs.readyState !== OPEN". Implementation uses "!this.wireWs || !this.joined" which is functionally equivalent because the close handler sets wireWs = null. A direct readyState check would catch the narrow window between WebSocket CLOSING state and close event handler.

Title: PR holding for operator review
Body: PR opened against main per release-validation-gate. Branch claude/d0030-wrapper-code-91c4. Files changed: worker/src/mcp.ts (271 insertions, 22 deletions). Journal committed.
